sourceMovie.title: Braveheart
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Braveheart (1995) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Mel Gibson's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Patriot
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Braveheart clicked for you, The Patriot (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Braveheart clicked for you, Free State of Jones (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Braveheart?
faq.answer: Try The Patriot, Free State of Jones, Rob Roy—each shares DNA with Braveheart while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Braveheart?
faq.answer: Braveheart (1995) is best described as Action, Drama, History, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Braveheart part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Braveheart Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
